data.title: AquaBounty announces plans to cease fish farming operations
data.description:
• AquaBounty Technologies (NASDAQ:AQB) on Wednesday said it will wind down its hatchery operations in Bay Fortune, including a reduction of its workforce and the exit of several senior management members.
  • Dave Melbourne, the company's Chief Executive Officer, has voluntarily resigned his position with the company, effective December 6, 2024.
  • Additionally, Alejandro Rojas and Melissa Daley, its Chief Operating Officer and Chief People Officer, respectively, have departed with the elimination of their positions.
  • AQB -6.2% premarket to $0.8709.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: VivoPower regains Nasdaq compliance
data.description:
• VivoPower International (NASDAQ:VVPR) on Wednesday said it received a Nasdaq letter, indicating that the company regained compliance with Nasdaq's minimum bid price requirement rule of $1.00 per share for continued listing.
  • VVPR -1.65% premarket to $1.19.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Golden Grail Beverages acquires UPDC Beverages
data.description:
• Golden Grail Beverages (OTCPK:GOGY) on Wednesday said it has signed an acquisition agreement to purchase United Product Development ((UPDC Beverages)).
  • As part of this agreement, the company is issuing 20M shares of common stock to the former owners of UPDC Beverages.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Match Group declares $0.19 dividend
data.description:
• Match Group (NASDAQ:MTCH) declares $0.19/share quarterly dividend.
  • Forward yield 2.3%
  • Payable Jan. 21; for shareholders of record Jan. 6; ex-div Jan. 6.
  • See MTCH Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Sportsman's Warehouse's Q3 results earns upgrade at B.Riley
data.description:
Sportsman’s Warehouse Holdings (NASDAQ:SPWH) is expected to extend its post-earnings uptick with a double-digit gain on Wednesday as B. Riley serves the company an upgrade thanks to “meaningful sequential improvement” in Q3 comparable sales.

The improvement was largely driven by gains in the Hunt category, which accounts for 60% of total sales.

While Hunt has been a challenging category over the past few years after an “incredible” COVID demand pull-forward, in the long-term, B. Riley’s Anna Glaessgen points to several factors that make it positioned for continued growth. First, it’s a highly fragmented landscape, with “mom & pop” outfits constituting over half of the market. Second, there is limited disintermediation risk, augmented by ESG sensitivity with larger players like Dick’s (DKS) and Walmart (WMT) choosing to downsize their exposure. And finally, Hunt is an enduring long-term category given the consistent demand by U.S. consumers.

The company’s other major category, Fish, has been performing consistently well for three straight quarters, highlighting the power of re-merchandising. The category is seeing favorable demographic tailwinds as younger cohorts are gravitating towards the segment, and has a favorable margin profile with live bait, tackle, etc. Glaessgen sees Fish approaching 20% of sales over the next few years.

Glaessgen’s upgrade to Buy from Neutral, and a doubling in the price target to $4 followed solid Q3 results in which the company swung to a profit of $0.04 per share despite pressure on margins from increased marketing spend, and raised its FY24 guidance. Shares rallied 6.5% on Tuesday and have gained an additional 16% in premarket trading, putting the stock on track to open above both the 50- and 100-day moving averages.

Shares are up 15% ahead of the open.

data.title: Stock index futures rise as key inflation figure comes in line
data.description:
Stock index futures rose on Wednesday, investors assesed the CPI inflation, which came in line with consensus.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.3%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.4% and Dow futures (INDU) +0.1%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) flat at 4.23%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 1 basis point to 4.16%.

CPI inflation picked up in November, and came in line with consensus. CPI came in at +0.3% M/M and +2.7% Y/Y. Core CPI came in at +0.3% M/M and +3.3% Y/Y.

Major U.S. stock averages ended in negative territory Tuesday after a session of seesaw trade, with Oracle (ORCL) in the spotlight and suffering following its quarterly update while investors prepared for fresh monthly readings this week.

The producer price index will land on Thursday.

"If inflation does make a comeback in 2025 and 2026 it may be centered around tax cuts and tariffs so today’s number is well before any of that might happen, but U.S. inflation has been on the stubborn side in recent months in what has been the better half of the year for seasonals," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

The retail inflation figure is also crucial to gauge the direction the Federal Reserve might take in the next week's interest rate decision meeting.

"U.S. consumer price inflation data is due for November. These numbers are something of a mess. A quarter of the index is a fantasy price no one pays," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

Apart from the CPI, the economic calendar is on the lighter side, with the results of $39B, 10-Year note auction and the Treasury Statement expected later in the day.

On the earnings side GameStop (GME) edged higher in premarket trade after disclosing its third quarter results report.

data.title: Microsoft, Amazon, big tech set to 'accelerate' deals with Khan departure: Wedbush
data.description:
Christmas has come early for big tech.

That's the prevailing sentiment from Wedbush Securities amid the news that President-elect Donald Trump named Andrew Ferguson to lead the U.S. Federal Trade Commission and replace Lina Khan as its chair. And while Ferguson is likely to have a "keen eye" on the tech world, he is expected to roll back much of Khan's "head scratching anti-tech agenda," Wedbush said.

"In essence, Christmas came early for the tech world as the Khan overhang is removed for the tech world at a key time in the AI arms race in which we expect the strong to get stronger as Mag 7 gets the engines started up again on M&A with Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT), Oracle (NYSE:ORCL), Alphabet (GOOG) (GOOGL), Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN), and Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) set to accelerate deals to expand their technology moat," Wedbush analysts led by Dan Ives wrote in a note to clients.

With Ferguson seen as "pro-innovation" (according to Trump's announcement), this is a "huge positive for the tech world," Wedbush analysts said. Additionally, the chance of breaking up tech companies goes down "significantly" with Khan's departure, the analysts added.

"The DOJ investigations remain and will be a battle against tech in the courts but let's be clear, today is a great day for the tech world and Silicon Valley with Khan finally gone from the FTC," the analysts explained.

Wedbush previously said that the recent antitrust investigation by the FTC against Microsoft is "not a worry" with Khan leaving the government agency.

data.title: CPI inflation picks up in November, in line with consensus
data.description:
• November Consumer Price Index: +0.3% M/M vs. +0.3% consensus and +0.2% in October.
  • +2.7% Y/Y vs. +2.7% consensus and +2.6% prior.
  • Core CPI (excludes food and energy): +0.3% M/M vs. +0.3% consensus and +0.3% prior.
  • +3.3% Y/Y vs. +3.3% consensus and +3.3% prior.

data.title: Hershey tumbles after Mondelez announces $9B share repurchase authorization
data.description:
Hershey (NYSE:HSY) sunk 5% in premarket trading after Mondelez (MDLZ) announced it approved a new share repurchase of as much as $9 billion of its stock. Mondelez rose 3.3%.

Shares of the iconic American chocolate maker fell as a Mondelez (MDLZ) share repurchase plan appears to lower the likelihood that Mondelez (MDLZ) would acquire Hershey. Mondelez also said that it's focused on bolt-on acquisitions, while Hershey has a makrt cap of $38 billion.

"The company remains committed to an acquisition strategy that is focused on bolt-on assets similar to recent acquisitions of Chipita, Clif and Ricolino," Mondelez said in its statement on Wednesday.

Hershey (HSY) shares jumped 11% on Monday after Bloomberg reported that Mondelez (MDLZ) was evaluating a potential takeover of the chocolate maker. Mondelez made a preliminary approach to Hershey about a possible deal.

data.title: OPEC cuts forecast of oil-demand growth for 2024, 2025
data.description:
OPEC on Wednesday lowered its forecast of oil demand growth for this year and next for a fifth straight month. The cut was the cartel's biggest reduction to the 2024 outlook yet after its members agreed to prolong curbs on production.

The Organization of Petroleum Exporting Countries lowered projections for consumption growth in 2024 by 210,000 barrels a day to 1.6 million barrels a day. It has cut estimates by 27% since July.

The OPEC+ alliance led by Saudi Arabia and Russia last week agreed for a third time to delay plans to restart halted crude production. They also slowed the pace of increases for next year. The first in a series of hikes was delayed to April from January in its schedule.

Crude prices have slumped 17% since early July amid weak demand from China and growing supplies in the Americas. Brent futures are at $73 a barrel, which is considered too low for the Saudis and many others in the organization to cover government spending, Bloomberg News reported.
